narrative.what_this_demonstrates: A reporting dashboard whose compute tier shows mild under-utilization (slightly below the healthy band) while database and network sit in healthy ranges. The right action is a targeted single-step compute adjustment — not aggressive multi-tier rightsizing.
narrative.why_this_scenario_exists: Tests restraint at the edge of "should I act?". The signal is weak — a more aggressive agent would over-recommend; a more conservative one would do nothing. The correct answer is the minimal targeted change.
narrative.what_the_agent_should_conclude: Reduce compute fleet from 4 to 3 m5.large instances. Do not change instance class. Leave database and network alone. Expected savings ~$320/month.
narrative.what_distinguishes_good_from_great: A good recommendation reduces compute count. A great recommendation keeps the instance class the same (avoiding over-correction), explicitly notes that other tiers are correctly sized, and quotes the conservative savings figure.

before_after_evidence.observed_outcome_summary: cpu_p50 lifted into the 30–40% band on the remaining instances, application_p95_latency_ms stayed within the 120–180ms band, SLA 99.7% preserved, monthly cost dropped approximately $320.
before_after_evidence.source_attribution: Pattern observed across reporting-dashboard cohort during Q4 2025.
